claim: Gemma 4 Edge models (2B and 4B) require 1-2GB RAM after quantization, making them suitable for on-device deployment on phones and IoT
actionable: Target Gemma 4 Edge models for devices with 1-2GB available RAM as a baseline requirement
source_title: Accelerating AI on Edge — Chintan Parikh and Weiyi Wang, Google DeepMind

claim: NPU acceleration can provide 3-10x performance improvement compared to CPU execution on edge devices, particularly for real-time AR/VR applications
actionable: Prioritize NPU support integration when deploying edge AI models for interactive applications requiring <100ms latency
source_title: Accelerating AI on Edge — Chintan Parikh and Weiyi Wang, Google DeepMind

claim: LiteRT (formerly TensorFlow Lite) supports cross-platform deployment across Android, iOS, macOS, Linux, Windows, web, and IoT with unified model format
actionable: Use LiteRT/TFLite format as the target model format to enable single-model deployment across all major platforms
source_title: Accelerating AI on Edge — Chintan Parikh and Weiyi Wang, Google DeepMind

claim: Gemma 4 Edge models support built-in function calling for tool use, structured JSON output, and chain-of-thought reasoning without additional prompt engineering
actionable: Leverage built-in structured output and function calling in Gemma 4 to reduce prompt engineering overhead for agentic applications
source_title: Accelerating AI on Edge — Chintan Parikh and Weiyi Wang, Google DeepMind

claim: Running LiteRT models on IoT devices with Raspberry Pi and CPU shows practical deployment is feasible for edge agents with latency acceptable for non-real-time tasks
actionable: Prototype edge agents on Raspberry Pi or similar constrained devices to validate deployment feasibility before production rollout
source_title: Accelerating AI on Edge — Chintan Parikh and Weiyi Wang, Google DeepMind

claim: Privacy benefits of on-device inference include avoiding data transmission to cloud servers, essential for handling sensitive customer data and documentation
actionable: Evaluate on-device deployment for workflows processing PII, healthcare records, or proprietary business documents to minimize data exposure
source_title: Accelerating AI on Edge — Chintan Parikh and Weiyi Wang, Google DeepMind
